implementation.pass_through_batching: The internal transformer combines contiguous row wrappers, raw text and structurally complete untouched cells into one callback-lifetime exact-byte span inside the current reader window.
implementation.audit_summary: Each batched action retains source cell count, independent coordinate extrema and shared-string, style and formula flags so PackageEditor can refresh dimensions and dependency diagnostics without replaying every cell action.
implementation.flush_boundaries: Replacement or insertion actions, worksheet metadata, unsupported detailed events, non-contiguous offsets and reader-window consumption flush the current span; no string_view survives a reader window.
implementation.attribute_scan: The reader extracts cell reference, shared-string and style attributes in one pass while preserving the prior malformed-source boundary after a valid cell reference.

interpretation.supported_claim: On this machine and the recorded one-million-cell level-1 targeted-upsert workloads, pass-through batching reduced transform actions and output append calls by 99.82%. Numeric and formula-plus-hyperlink total editor medians decreased by 21.21% and 15.69%; mixed-inline total median changed from 1205 ms to 1222 ms and is treated as unchanged rather than a throughput gain.
interpretation.compatibility: All representative outputs opened with openpyxl 3.1.2, the formula profile preserved the recorded external hyperlink target, and final production CTest passed 132/132.
interpretation.memory: Median process peak working set decreased by 0.21-0.22 MB and the transform output pending buffer stayed below 29 KiB in the recorded profiles.
interpretation.next_hotspot: After action and append traffic fell to roughly 2100 calls, 0.44-0.63 seconds remained in source scan/action time. Mixed-inline is now the priority parser fast-path profile; temporary output IO remains secondary.

scope: Single Windows/MSVC machine, one-million-cell 10-column numeric, mixed-inline and formula distributions, 1000 targeted upserts, source DEFLATE level 6 and output DEFLATE level 1. No cross-machine, arbitrary-workbook, Office, full-feature or OpenXLSX-superiority claim.
